libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, external AI service, API key, environment variable, or system property is required. Use a local stub ChatModel/ApplicationContextRunner-style test setup to exercise the auto-configuration. For 2.0.0, do not use the removed RC2-only stream-tool-call-responses property or isStreamToolCallResponses() accessor.

deterministic_setup.reason: The auto-configuration is conditional on and builds Spring AI ChatClient/ChatModel types; this dependency is optional in the artifact POM, so tests need it explicitly.
The chat client auto-configuration is ordered after ToolCallingAutoConfiguration and has tool-calling advisor paths that require the tool auto-configuration classes for meaningful coverage.
The tested classes are Spring Boot auto-configuration classes using Boot conditional/configuration annotations, but Boot autoconfigure is optional in the artifact POM.
Spring Boot auto-configuration coverage is normally exercised with ApplicationContextRunner, which is supplied by spring-boot-test.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8744 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-autoconfigure-model-chat-client:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing RC2 test build pins helper dependencies to RC2/4.1.0-RC1; leaving those pins while testing 2.0.0 can reproduce javac failures from API drift.
Adding optional tracing dependencies is not required for baseline coverage and may change which observation configuration branch Spring Boot activates.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: This is a Spring Boot auto-configuration artifact whose meaningful coverage depends on optional Spring AI and Spring Boot classes that Maven/Gradle will not bring in transitively from the tested artifact.
